title: He Reproves The Curlew
content:
O Curlew, cry no more in the air,
Or only to the water in the West;
Because your crying brings to my mind
passion-dimmed eyes and long heavy hair
That was shaken out over my breast:
There is enough evil in the crying of wind.
meaning:
William Butler Yeats's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "He Reproves The Curlew"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
